name: Stuffed Pork Tenderloin
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Lay the pork tenderloin on a chopping board and trim off most of the fat.
3. Score two vertical lines down the length of it, taking care not to pierce through the meat. Place the pork between two pieces of plastic wrap and pound the meat lightly with a flat mallet so that it forms a shape somewhere between an oval and a rectangle.
4. Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in a saucepan and add the mushrooms. Cook for 2-3 minutes until soft.
5. Remove the sausage meat from its casing and put it into a small mixing bowl. Add the mushrooms, parsley, and thyme and blend well. Stir in the egg to bind.
6. Spoon the sausage meat mixture down the center of the pork. Fold over the two end flaps of the pork and then the sides to form a roll. Place the slices of bacon on top. Tie the roll in 2 or 3 places with string.
7. Place the roll on a sheet of foil and dot the remaining butter on top. Wrap the foil around the pork roll and set on a baking sheet.
8. Cook for 30 minutes. Remove the foil, baste the pork with the cooking juices, and cook for a further 10 minutes.
9. Remove and discard the string and bacon. Cut the meat into slices crosswise. Pour the cooking juices into a small saucepan, add the wine, and bring to a boil. Serve this sauce with the pork.
This dish: Paupiette de porc
